check: ★ the FLAT cap is a real edge (it does not refine away)
value: seam crease 91.9 -> 92.1 -> 92.1 deg over the ladder; exponent q = -0.00
criterion: > 80 deg at EVERY level; q ~ 0 (no decay whatsoever)
note: a sharp convex edge creases by its own turning angle; halving h RESOLVES it and removes nothing. This is the object P13/G13.3 measured DIVERGING in the flow (p = +0.321). Note the MEDIAN seam crease is 0.00 deg -- the rest of the seam is already smooth; so this metric is reading the edge and nothing else

check: ★ the ROUND cap creases like O(h) => no edge in the limit
criterion: q <= -0.7 -- the crease DECAYS with h; i.e. there is no edge in the limit
note: facets approximating a SMOOTH surface crease by O(h * curvature). Fitted on the p99; not the max: the max is a single worst facet at the thin end of the seam window (where the cap radius is smallest and the facet size is set by h_edge; not h_tip); so it decays more slowly (46.8 -> 25.0 -> 18.1) while the p99 halves cleanly. Both DECAY -- which is the claim; the flat cap's does not move at all

check: the cap is resolved to its apex
value: coarse: wall z_max 1.218465; medium: wall z_max 1.218466; fine: wall z_max 1.218467 (apex 1.218467)
criterion: reaches the apex to within 1% of the cap radius
note: cap radius = the tip section's max half thickness; 22.2 mm = 1.9% of the semi-span; h_tip = 0.25 h_wall exists so the coarse cap is not one element wide (which would discretize it back flat)

check: NOTHING ELSE MOVED (coarse): TE corner + wake sheet
value: tip TE corner offset 0.00e+00 m; wake |y|max 2.37e-15; z_max 1.196300
criterion: corner exact; wake in the chord plane; ending at B_SEMI
note: the cap radius vanishes at the TE; so it degenerates to a point exactly at the corner the wake attaches to. That is what makes the M1 A/B controlled

criterion: tip TE corner stays a FREE edge node (Gamma(tip) = 0 discretely)

check: element quality inside the M1 bounds
value: coarse: min dihedral 4.05 deg; max aspect 16.2; medium: min dihedral 5.18 deg; max aspect 6.4; fine: min dihedral 8.63 deg; max aspect 6.4
note: a small-radius feature does cost some quality; the gate is that it stays inside the bounds the solver has always run on

check: COST: the cap refinement; and it is level-independent
criterion: recorded; the RATIO must be level-independent or the ladder is not self-similar
note: h_tip scales with h_wall; so the cap costs the same FRACTION at every level -- the refinement ray is preserved. Measured against M1's SELF-SIMILAR coarse (coarse_ss); against its shipped clamped `coarse` the ratio would read a spurious 1.07; because that mesh has an over-refined far field of its own
